page: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features 64 dedicated physical cores utilizing the x86_64 AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, relying instead on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier performance in multi-core processing and memory bandwidth. The instance achieves top 10% placement in stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, Geekbench multi-core scores, and PassMark CPU physics evaluations. Memory subsystem performance is strong, with small-block cached read bandwidth ranking in the top 10% and large-block uncached read/write speeds placing in the top 25%. In specialized workloads, the server demonstrates top-tier capabilities in software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and image processing.

From a resource density perspective, the c7a.16xlarge offers high compute density with dedicated physical cores, which eliminates hyperthreading resource sharing. However, the 2.0 GB of RAM per core represents a lower memory-to-CPU ratio, which may limit memory-bound applications. The lack of local NVMe SSD storage requires reliance on network storage, introducing potential latency tradeoffs. The hardware profile delivers high qualitative cost efficiency for compute-heavy tasks due to its DDR5 memory speeds and advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration.

This instance is configured for workloads requiring intensive parallel processing and high clock speeds. Appropriate applications include software compilation, ray tracing, image processing, and database operations such as Redis, where it achieves top-tier SET operation throughput. It is also suitable for medium-scale LLM inference tasks, demonstrating strong to top-tier prompt processing and text generation speeds.
description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R14 processor running at 3.7 GHz. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, it includes 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, yielding a 2.0 GB per core ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, utilizing a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth instead. Benchmark results place this server in the top 10% for multi-core CPU performance, software compilation, ray tracing, and Redis SET operations. It also demonstrates strong memory bandwidth and efficient LLM inference capabilities. The dedicated core configuration provides high compute density, though the lower memory-to-core ratio presents a tradeoff for memory-intensive applications. This server is suited for compute-heavy workloads, including software compilation, image processing, database operations, and parallel simulation tasks.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and 25 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7a.16xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 64 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of DDR5 memory.
tagline: A compute-dense platform featuring sixty-four dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high-speed DDR5 memory for parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: 64 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R14 physical cores running at 3.7 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 5 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier multi-core CPU, ray tracing, and software compilation performance
25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Suited for parallel computing, database operations, and LLM inference
